title: ScopeDefinition
description: Filesystem access boundaries for an agent. Enforced at two layers: the scope middleware intercepts tool calls (Read, Glob, Grep, Bash) targeting paths outside the declared scope, and the CWD env var provides an OS-level hard floor. All paths are relative to the target project root (CWD).

readWrite.description: Glob patterns the agent can both read and modify. E.g. ['src/components/**', 'src/styles/**', 'src/pages/**']. The agent's primary working territory.

readOnly.description: Glob patterns the agent can read but not modify. Provides context without write risk. E.g. ['src/api/**', 'package.json', 'tsconfig.json']. Tool calls that attempt to write to readOnly paths are rejected with an explanation.

forbidden.description: Glob patterns the agent cannot access at all — not even read. E.g. ['.env', '**/*.pem', '**/*.key', 'server/src/**']. These paths are invisible to the agent. Tool calls targeting them return 'path not found' (not 'access denied') to avoid leaking the existence of sensitive files.

globalForbidden.description: Paths forbidden for ALL agents regardless of role. Merged with the per-agent forbidden list. Protects credentials, keys, and sensitive system directories. Can be extended in runtime-config.json but never reduced.

escalationPath.description: 'bus': when the agent needs a file outside its scope, the middleware posts a request to the Lead via the bus and the Lead returns the relevant excerpt. 'reject': access is denied with no fallback. Default 'bus' for readOnly/forbidden files; 'reject' for globalForbidden.
